About
# About CostHawk

CostHawk gives engineering teams real-time visibility into AI API spend across every provider, model, and project. Start with a 30-day trial, then move to a paid plan. Built by an engineer who needed it and couldn't find it.

[Start free trial](https://costhawk.ai/sign-up)[Read the Docs](https://docs.costhawk.ai/)

Glossary

58

terms defined in the AI cost glossary.

Models Tracked

196

models with live pricing data indexed.

Providers

13

AI provider families supported today.

Pricing

From $19/mo

30-day trial on Individual and Team.

Origin Story

## Built because the bill was a surprise every month

CostHawk started inside TrainMyAgent, where our engineering team was shipping AI features fast and getting blindsided by API bills that were two or three times what we expected. We had no breakdown by model, no idea which projects were burning through tokens, and no warning before the invoice hit.

I looked for a tool that could just show me what we were spending, broken down by provider and project, in real time. Everything I found was either enterprise-locked, buried behind a sales call, or only covered a single provider. So I built one.

CostHawk is the tool I wished existed: one dashboard for every AI provider, with local-first telemetry so you don't have to hand over admin credentials to get started.

Founder

### Chase Dillingham

Founder

Full-stack engineer and founder of CostHawk. Previously building AI products at TrainMyAgent, where the need for real-time cost visibility became impossible to ignore.

Company

### Outperform Ventures LLC

dba TrainMyAgent

CostHawk is a product of Outperform Ventures LLC, the same team behind TrainMyAgent.

AI Cost Monitoring

## Start tracking your AI spend in minutes

Install the MCP, connect your first provider, and see exactly where your tokens are going. Start with a 30-day trial; paid plans begin at $19/month.

[Start free trial](https://costhawk.ai/sign-up)[Read the Docs](https://docs.costhawk.ai/)

CostHawk gives leadership teams one operating view across AI adoption, tracked provider spend, and equivalent local-tool value.
